meta.description: Common DNA across all SOTY winners 2020-2024 — patterns, scoring analysis, and gap analysis for Event Horizon

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 4 out of 5 SOTY winners (2020-2024) used WebGL. Exception: Pangram Pangram 2022.
recurringPatterns.details: Three.js is the most common foundation, but winners always customize heavily — custom shaders, custom render pipelines, custom post-processing. Using Three.js out of the box is NOT enough.

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Event Horizon uses Three.js with GPGPU compute and custom raymarching shaders. This is highly customized, not vanilla Three.js.

recurringPatterns.prevalence: All WebGL winners (4/5) had bespoke GLSL. The shader work IS what makes each site unique.
recurringPatterns.details: Generic post-processing (bloom, DOF, etc.) is table stakes. Winners have NOVEL shader techniques — cloth playback, SDF text, ping-pong compositing, crystal PBR, CGI compositing. The custom shader is often the Developer Award justification.

winners.2023_Lusion: Cloth playback vertex shader, custom post-processing chain
winners.2024_Igloo: SDF text shader, crystal PBR with subsurface scattering, VDB raymarching

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Raymarched black hole with gravitational lensing, photon sphere rendering, accretion disk emission — all custom GLSL. GPGPU N-body compute shader. This is the strongest technical differentiator.
eventHorizonMapping.gap: Ensure the shaders are VISUALLY stunning, not just technically correct. Judges see the visual, not the code.

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 5 out of 5 SOTY winners use scroll as the primary interaction driver.
recurringPatterns.details: Every SOTY winner tells a story through scroll. The scroll IS the interaction model. But each implements it differently — some hijack scroll completely, others use smooth scroll libraries, one (Pangram) uses Locomotive Scroll. The key: scroll drives a narrative, not just reveals content.

winners.2020_Pioneer: Scroll → timeline progress → CGI scene progression
winners.2023_Lusion: Scroll → cloth animation playback + camera movement
winners.2024_Igloo: Scroll → crystal growth + camera spline path

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Scroll drives the approach to the black hole — user scrolls toward the event horizon. Scroll position maps to gravitational proximity.
eventHorizonMapping.gap: The narrative arc needs to be as strong as the visual arc. What STORY does the scroll tell? Scientific discovery? Personal journey? Cosmic insignificance? Define the narrative clearly.
eventHorizonMapping.recommendation: Scroll = approaching the black hole. Each section = new astrophysical concept revealed. Climax = crossing the event horizon. Resolution = what lies beyond (theoretical).

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 5 out of 5 SOTY winners have custom or smooth scroll implementations.
recurringPatterns.details: Native browser scroll is never used as-is. Every winner either hijacks scroll entirely (mapping to a virtual scroll position) or uses a smooth scroll library. The smoothness of scrolling IS a quality signal to judges.

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Event Horizon needs buttery smooth scroll that feels connected to the gravitational physics metaphor.
eventHorizonMapping.gap: Consider: scroll momentum could increase as user gets closer to the black hole (time dilation = scroll feels different). The scroll physics should CHANGE as the narrative progresses.
eventHorizonMapping.recommendation: Use Lenis or GSAP ScrollSmoother as base, customize with gravitational scroll physics: far = normal, close = accelerating (gravity pulls you in), event horizon = scroll becomes irreversible.

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 5 out of 5 SOTY winners maintain 60fps on target devices (or have intentional framerate variations like Prometheus).
recurringPatterns.details: Any frame drop is noticed by judges. Performance optimization is not optional — it's the foundation. Winners invest heavily in: frame budget monitoring, adaptive quality, LOD systems, efficient rendering pipelines, and performance testing across devices.

winners.2020_Pioneer: Pre-rendered CGI approach avoids GPU bottlenecks
winners.2021_Prometheus: Intentional 12fps was artistic, but compositing always 60fps

eventHorizonMapping.detail: GPGPU N-body simulation + raymarched black hole + particles + post-processing = heavy GPU load. 60fps is the biggest technical challenge.
eventHorizonMapping.gap: High risk of frame drops on mid-range hardware. MUST implement aggressive adaptive quality.
eventHorizonMapping.recommendation: Adaptive quality system: (1) Monitor frame time rolling average. (2) If >16.6ms: reduce particle count → lower raymarching steps → reduce render resolution → simplify post-processing. (3) Never let judges see a stutter.

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 4 out of 5 SOTY winners have sound design. Exception: Pangram Pangram 2022 (intentional silence).
recurringPatterns.details: Sound is not background music — it's synchronized with the visual experience. Scroll-triggered audio, spatial positioning, volume tied to interaction. Sound ENHANCES the narrative.

winners.2020_Pioneer: Ambient soundtrack + scroll-triggered effects via Howler.js
winners.2021_Prometheus: Warm/cold audio matching visual modes, spatial panning
winners.2022_Pangram: No sound (intentional — typography is the voice)
winners.2023_Lusion: Fabric sounds synced with cloth animation, spatial audio
winners.2024_Igloo: Crystal resonance sounds, procedural audio, spatial positioning

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Sound design is non-negotiable for Event Horizon. The theme demands it.
eventHorizonMapping.gap: No sound design mentioned in current implementation.
eventHorizonMapping.recommendation: Physics-based sound design: (1) Silence of space in opening. (2) Low-frequency rumble growing as black hole approaches. (3) Doppler-shifted tones for orbiting particles. (4) Overwhelming sub-bass at event horizon. (5) Hawking radiation as high-frequency digital noise. (6) Time dilation = audio pitch-shifting and time-stretching. (7) Inside the black hole = reversed, distorted audio suggesting information paradox.

recurringPatterns.prevalence: 5 out of 5 SOTY winners have at least one moment/technique that makes you say 'I didn't know a browser could do that.'
recurringPatterns.details: This is the most important pattern. Every SOTY winner has a UNIQUE innovation that defines the project. It's never just excellent execution of known techniques — it's something NEW.

winners.2024_Igloo: Full WebGL UI with zero DOM text (rendering innovation)

eventHorizonMapping.detail: Event Horizon has multiple potential 'holy shit' moments: (1) Real-time GPGPU N-body gravitational simulation with thousands of particles. (2) Raymarched black hole with accurate gravitational lensing. (3) Real astrophysics data visualization. (4) Time dilation as UX (framerate + scroll speed change near singularity).
eventHorizonMapping.gap: The innovations must be VISIBLE and VISCERAL. Technical complexity alone doesn't win — the judge must FEEL it.
eventHorizonMapping.recommendation: The 'holy shit' moment should be: scrolling past the event horizon and seeing reality warp — the entire webpage bends, stretches, the background stars gravitationally lens around the black hole, particles accelerate to visible velocity. The physics are real, the feeling is overwhelming.

overall.trend: Increasing year over year — the bar rises

design.trend: Design scores consistently highest — it's the most valued category at 40% weight

usability.trend: Usability is typically the lowest score — immersive experiences sacrifice some usability. But Pangram Pangram proved high usability can win.

creativity.trend: Creativity scores have the widest range. WebGL innovations score highest. Pangram Pangram's lower creativity score was offset by exceptional design and usability.

content.trend: Content importance increasing — judges want meaningful content, not just pretty visuals

targetScoresForEventHorizon.design: 9.3+ — must match or exceed Igloo's visual quality
targetScoresForEventHorizon.usability: 8.8+ — immersive but navigable, clear user path
targetScoresForEventHorizon.creativity: 9.5+ — the raymarched black hole + GPGPU should push this high
targetScoresForEventHorizon.content: 9.0+ — real astrophysics content elevates beyond 'tech demo'

goodSite.description: Beautifully designed, well-coded, nice animations, smooth performance
goodSite.missing: Nothing UNIQUE. It's excellent execution of known techniques.

greatSite.description: Stunning design, novel interactions, strong narrative, technical sophistication
greatSite.missing: The innovation doesn't push the medium forward. It's the best version of something that exists.

exceptionalSite.description: Redefines expectations, has a 'holy shit' moment, tells a compelling story, technically innovative, beautifully designed
exceptionalSite.missing: Consistency — maybe mobile is weaker, maybe one section isn't as polished, maybe the innovation doesn't sustain throughout.

sotyWinner.description: Everything above PLUS: the innovation sustains the entire experience, every detail is perfected, mobile is strong, sound enhances, loading delights, and it makes people FEEL something they haven't felt in a browser before.
requirements: At least one genuinely novel technique
Sustained quality from first pixel to last
Strong narrative that gives meaning to the spectacle
Mobile experience that maintains the essence
Sound design that enhances (or intentional silence that speaks)
Loading experience that sets the tone
Technical innovation that the community can learn from
Emotional impact — judges REMEMBER it months later

wowFactorAnalysis.definition: The 'wow factor' is the moment when a judge leans forward, involuntarily. It's not planned — it's a physical reaction to seeing something they didn't think was possible.

whatTriggersWow.description: Seeing something in a browser that looks like it should require a dedicated app or rendering software
whatTriggersWow.eventHorizonPotential: Raymarched black hole with accurate gravitational lensing — looks like Interstellar in a browser

whatTriggersWow.description: Real-time feedback that feels magical — the site responds to you in ways you didn't expect
whatTriggersWow.eventHorizonPotential: Cursor creates gravitational influence on particles — your mouse IS a gravity source

whatTriggersWow.description: A moment where the scale of the experience suddenly becomes apparent — zoom in/out reveals unexpected depth
examples: Igloo's crystal formation revealing macro structure
whatTriggersWow.eventHorizonPotential: Starting at cosmic scale, zooming into the black hole to atomic scale, revealing Hawking radiation particles

whatTriggersWow.description: The story takes an unexpected turn that's enhanced by the interactive medium
examples: Prometheus's mode switch from collage to 3D
whatTriggersWow.eventHorizonPotential: Crossing the event horizon — the entire visual paradigm inverts. What was outside is now inside. Time flows differently. The narrative breaks the fourth wall.

whatTriggersWow.description: Multiple senses engaged simultaneously — visual + audio + interaction creates a total immersion moment
whatTriggersWow.eventHorizonPotential: Deep sub-bass vibration + visual gravitational lensing + scroll acceleration near event horizon — total immersion in the gravity well

eventHorizonWowMoments.moment: First sight of the raymarched black hole
eventHorizonWowMoments.description: User scrolls and the black hole comes into view — genuine gravitational lensing warping the starfield behind it. Accretion disk glowing. This should look like Interstellar or better.
Moving the cursor near the black hole causes particles to orbit/accrete. The physics are real — it's not just animation, it's simulation.

The defining moment. Everything changes — visuals invert, time dilates (framerate drops intentionally), scroll behavior changes (can't scroll back — like real black holes, information doesn't escape).
Near the singularity, the viewport itself stretches vertically — as if the screen is being pulled into the singularity. The browser chrome seems to distort.

innovationUniquenessRequirement.principle: Every SOTY winner brings something the web has never seen before. It's not enough to do existing things better — you must do something NEW.

technicalNovelty.description: A new rendering technique, data format, or engineering approach
technicalNovelty.eventHorizonCandidate: GPGPU N-body gravitational simulation + raymarched black hole with accurate lensing

conceptualNovelty.description: A new way of thinking about web experiences — reframing what a website can be
conceptualNovelty.eventHorizonCandidate: Real astrophysics as interactive experience — black hole physics as UX metaphor

experientialNovelty.description: A new FEELING delivered through the browser — emotional territory unexplored in web
examples: The warmth of analog in digital (Prometheus)
experientialNovelty.eventHorizonCandidate: The sublime terror of cosmic scale — the feeling of approaching something from which nothing escapes

innovationUniquenessRequirement.eventHorizonUniqueValue: No Awwwards winner has ever: (1) raymarched a black hole in a browser, (2) used real gravitational physics as the interaction model, (3) visualized real astrophysical phenomena (lensing, accretion, Hawking radiation) interactively, (4) used time dilation as a UX device. This is genuinely novel on multiple axes.

categories.sotyStandard: At least one novel technique that pushes the medium
categories.detail: GPGPU N-body + raymarching = multiple novel techniques
categories.action: None needed — this is the strongest area

categories.detail: Raymarched black hole must look physically accurate AND visually stunning
categories.action: Reference Interstellar's black hole (Kip Thorne's equations). The visual benchmark is Christopher Nolan, not Three.js demos.

categories.sotyStandard: Meaningful story that gives context to the spectacle
categories.detail: Tech demo vs. experience. Must have real astrophysics content — teach the user about black holes.
categories.action: Write narrative content: what is a black hole, event horizon, gravitational lensing, Hawking radiation, spaghettification, information paradox. Each section of the scroll experience maps to a concept.
Implement physics-based sound: sub-bass for gravity, Doppler for orbiting particles, time-stretch for dilation, silence for space

categories.sotyStandard: Engaging preloader that sets the tone
categories.detail: Heavy assets (GPGPU shaders, textures, HDR) will need significant load time
categories.action: Design a preloader that IS the experience: stars appearing one by one, forming a distant view of the black hole, then zooming in as loading completes

categories.detail: GPGPU + raymarching is extremely heavy on mobile GPUs
categories.action: Dedicated mobile version: simplified black hole (mesh-based instead of raymarched), fewer particles, native scroll, touch-optimized interactions

categories.sotyStandard: Navigable, understandable, doesn't lose the user
categories.detail: Immersive black hole experience must not confuse users
categories.action: Clear navigation, progress indication, ability to skip/restart sections, visible controls

categories.sotyStandard: 60fps on target devices, adaptive quality for weaker devices
categories.detail: Heaviest GPU load of any potential SOTY candidate
categories.action: Implement frame budget monitoring with aggressive adaptive quality. Pre-computed fallbacks for weaker devices. LOD for particles.

categories.sotyStandard: Basic accessibility (keyboard nav, screen reader content, reduced motion support)
categories.action: Hidden DOM layer for screen readers, prefers-reduced-motion support (static black hole image fallback), keyboard navigation, high contrast mode

categories.sotyStandard: Technical writeup that helps the community learn
categories.detail: Developer Award winners publish technical breakdowns
categories.action: Write a detailed technical article: how the GPGPU N-body works, how the raymarching achieves accurate lensing, performance optimization strategies. Publish on Medium/personal blog before submission.

overallReadiness.verdict: Strong technical foundation but significant gaps in narrative, sound, mobile, and documentation. Technical innovation is SOTY-caliber but the complete experience needs more work.
overallReadiness.timeEstimate: 4-8 weeks of dedicated work to close all gaps
